form_analysis.form_header_text: FY27 Appropriations Request Form (Programmatic and Language Requests Only)
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Congress will soon begin the annual process of allocating funding levels to support government operations for Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27). We welcome your input in identifying federal programs that are most important to the constituents of Maryland's 7th Congressional District. This form can be used for both programmatic funding requests in addition to language requests. Please note that submission of this form does not mean that the proposal will be funded. Requests must be merit-based and comply with House rules. If you have multiple requests, please submit a separate form for each request. Please feel free to contact MD07ProgramLanguage@mail.house.gov for assistance. This form is for programmatic and language requests only. Information on how to apply for Community Project Funding is available through a separate form. Community Project Funding is defined as federal funding that is directed to a specific government entity, non-profit organization, or geographic area. If you have questions about Community Project Funding, please feel free to contact Austen Edelenbos at MD07CommunityProject@mail.house.gov for assistance.

sections.description: General details about the federal program and request type.

fields.label: Priority Level (if submitting multiple requests)
fields.help_text: Assign a rank from 1 (highest priority) to 10.
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is a submitter-specific determination and cannot be inferred from the provided context.

fields.label: Agency
fields.rationale: Standard agency name from reference values.

fields.label: Program
fields.rationale: Standard program name used for SMD funding requests as per instructions.

fields.label: Account
fields.rationale: The 'Science' account is the specific appropriations account for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Other Offices Request Made To
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Type of Request
fields.rationale: The request context specifies this is a programmatic funding request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Program Funding
options: Support the President's FY27 Budget Request
fields.rationale: The request seeks $9 billion, which is a $1.75 billion increase (24%) over the FY2026 enacted level of $7.25 billion.

fields.label: FY27 Request Amount
fields.rationale: This is the specific funding ask provided in the reference values for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: President's FY27 Budget Request
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not yet been released as noted in the SMD Topline Funding History.

fields.label: FY26 Enacted Level
fields.rationale: This value is provided as the enacted total for FY2026 in the reference data.

fields.label: President's FY26 Budget Request
fields.rationale: The FY2026 President's Budget Request (PBR) for SMD was $3.908 billion. The pre-filled value of $7.25B appears to be the Enacted amount, not the PBR.

fields.label: Description of Program
fields.draft_value:
* NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) manages over 100 missions exploring the Earth, the solar system, and the deep universe based on National Academies' Decadal Survey priorities.
* This $9 billion programmatic request restores funding for critical missions following FY 2026 disruptions, ensuring the U.S. maintains its global leadership in space science against rising international competition.
* SMD supports a robust innovation pipeline, funding over 10,000 scientists and engineers who develop advanced technologies in remote sensing, data science, and aerospace engineering.
fields.rationale: This value describes the core functions of the NASA Science Mission Directorate as requested in the funding ask, emphasizing the strategic importance and the $9B request level.

fields.label: Relevance to MD-07
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science is a primary economic engine for MD-07, with over $964 million in obligations within the district between FY 2023 and FY 2025.
* The Association of Universities for Research in Astronomy (AURA) in Baltimore manages the science operations for the Hubble, James Webb, and Roman Space Telescopes under major contracts.
* Local academic institutions, including UMBC ($160.7M in grants) and Johns Hopkins University ($36.3M in grants), lead critical research in Earth science and heliophysics.
fields.rationale: This draft cites specific spending data and high-value contract/grant recipients located in MD-07, including AURA, UMBC, and Johns Hopkins, as provided in the NASA spending context.

fields.label: Subcommittee Jurisdiction
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: The NASA Science Mission Directorate is under the jurisdiction of the CJS Subcommittee.

fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request; the reference values state 'N/A' for both report and bill language, making this section non-applicable.

fields.label: Text of Proposed Language Request
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request for an existing program; the provided context indicates that no specific bill or report language is being requested for this submission.

fields.label: Description of Program
fields.rationale: This field appears to be a duplicate or part of a different request section (e.g., Language or Project funding) that does not apply to this programmatic request for NASA SMD.

fields.label: Program Element Number & Line Number
fields.rationale: NASA's budget structure typically identifies the 'Science' account as the primary line item for these activities within the CJS bill.

top_contracts.recipient_name: Association of Universities for Research in Astronomy, Inc.
top_contracts.description: The primary activities covered by this effort include the final servicing mission and subsequent operations through end-of-mission. These activities include: a. Science program implementation including: mission planning support, science program definition support, management of the proposal selection, time allocation, and grant award committee processes, and science community outreach. B. Routine science operations including: proposal generation and receipt, observation planning and scheduling, updating instrument handbooks, observing and user support, data processing and product generation, archival and data distribution services, calibration assistance and data analysis, anomaly investigation support, user grant administration, observer assistance and engineering operations support. C. Ground system sustaining engineering including: science operation system maintenance, infrastructure administration and support, and documentation maintenance d. Servicing mission support including: preparation and execution of the observatory verification program, instrument development support, and optical alignment testing support E. Generation of performance, science return, and other pertinent informational reports to the hstp f. Astronomical community and general public/educational outreach g. Special projects including origins forum and independent research this contract includes an additional two-year period after the end of hst'S science-mission.  This extra time following the cessation of scientific observations is for two years of follow-on archival research. During the final two years of the contract, archival operations and maintenance, an archival research proposal selection process and associated grant administration, and a public outreach program will continue. Data analysis software will also be maintained but at a lower level of support such that only modest enhancements may still be made.  Continuing support will also include the coordination of data analysis software that is produced by the astronomical community.  Orig psc ar20
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Ensuring decades of Hubble Space Telescope scientific discovery and data access.

top_contracts.recipient_name: Association of Universities for Research in Astronomy, Inc.
top_contracts.description: The primary activities covered by this effort span the major phases of the mission beginning with formulation through development and testing, and ending one year after launch with the initiation of the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) science program. These major activities are separated into the baseline program and special studies. The baseline program includes: a. Mission requirements analysis and observatory/mission design support including: tradestudy generation, science and operations impact analysis, science systems engineering support, and design reference mission analysis. B. Ground segment development, testing, maintenance and operations including: operations support of si, fgs, isim, and observatory Integration and Test (I&T), mission simulations, and documentation maintenance, as well as low cost operations concepts. C. Science program implementation including: mission planning support, science program definition support, proposal management and user grant administration, observer assistance, science community outreach, and staff research. D. Commissioning period and routine science operations including: proposal generation, observation planning and scheduling, observing and user support, data processing and product generation, archival and data distribution services, calibration assistance and data analysis, and engineering operations support. E. Flight operations including: subsystem (including instruments) health and safety monitoring, command generation and upload, schedule execution assurance, trending, and anomaly investigation. F. Support and execution of the on-orbit commissioning program. G. Generation of performance, science return, and other pertinent informational reports to the JWST project. H. Astronomical community and general public/educational outreach. Examples of special project activities may include: a. Follow-on mission studies. B. Engineering necessary to respond to new JWST project tasks.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Funding the full lifecycle of James Webb Space Telescope science operations.

top_contracts.recipient_name: Association of Universities for Research in Astronomy, Inc.
top_contracts.description: This contract is for the products and services associated with the design phase of the wide-field infrared survey telescope (WFIRST) Science Operations Center (SOC)
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Building the operational control center for the Roman Space Telescope.

top_contracts.description: Geospace Dynamics Constellation (GDC) modular spectrometer for atmosphere and ionosphere characterization (MOSAIC) instrument investigation.  Original ao nnh17zda004o-gdc
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Characterizing Earth's upper atmosphere using a new GDC instrument.

top_grants.description: The proposed gestar ii consortium comprises five top-ranked universities and three non-academic.partners: university of maryland  baltimore county (umbc  prime)  morgan state university.(gestar incumbent)  colorado state university  pennsylvania stat
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Building academic research capacity through the Gestar II university consortium.
Funding critical research protecting Earth from space weather impacts.
Developing a solar coronal ejection tracker instrument.

top_grants.description: Lems-a3: the lunar environment monitoring station for Artemis 3
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Establishing environmental monitoring hardware for Artemis 3.

top_grants.description: Minicor miniature coronagraph for heliophysics research & operations
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Building a miniature solar instrument for heliophysics research.
